forecasts.reasoning: The state of emergency (SOE) declared on March 31, 2025, was specifically and widely reported to be in effect for 30 days, expiring on April 29, 2025. This expiration date is exactly one day before the target date of April 30. Crucially, as of April 21, 2025, the Electric Power Corporation (EPC) officially cancelled the power rationing program that had necessitated the SOE, citing the successful installation of temporary rental generators. While permanent generators are not expected until August 2025, the immediate 'emergency' justifying the constitutional declaration appears to have subsided. Additionally, government notices regarding tax exemptions for energy equipment were specifically tied to the April 29 deadline. Given the cessation of rationing and the lack of any announced extension by the knowledge cutoff, it is more likely than not that the SOE will be allowed to lapse as scheduled.

Multiple highly credible sources (Axios, Politico, Punchbowl News, and The Washington Post) indicate that Rep. Haley Stevens decided to enter the race for the open Michigan Senate seat vacated by Senator Gary Peters, who announced his retirement in January 2025. Reports on April 10, 2025, stated she was 'imminently' launching her campaign, and a Washington Post article from April 21, 2025, confirms she has entered the race. Her social media also reflects this announcement. The timing is well before the May 1, 2025, deadline.
